policy_reminder: A published benchmark recommendation is evidence, not an automatic production change. Nothing below alters router tier assignments; promotion requires explicit operator action.
production_impact: NONE. Production heavy (gpt-oss-120b) and fast (qwen36-35b-a3b) tiers are unchanged and were restored after the bakeoff window.

recommendations.status: best measured candidate for the role; NOT promoted
recommendations.highlights: official NVIDIA checkpoint; preflight ALL PASS; full 131k sweep; intelligence 2/2; 137 tok/s with MTP n=3 (1.50x verified A/B)
caveats: measured on vLLM nightly v0.23.1rc1 — pin a stable release before any role
prefix caching unavailable with MTP on this architecture (engine auto-disables; upstream #39809)
card's 262k+ context claims unverified here (131k verified)

recommendations.role: heavy / quality + agentic (superseded by the 2026-07-11 extension)
recommendations.status: superseded: best measured heavy candidate is now Nemotron-Labs-3-Puzzle-75B (see below); MiniMax REAP remains a strong community-checkpoint alternative
recommendations.highlights: clean sweep with thinking disabled (intelligence 2/2, tools, session, 64k), 97.2 tok/s single-stream, TTFT p50 86 ms warm
caveats: community REAP-pruned checkpoint, not an official MiniMax release — provenance and long-tail quality unverified
94.3 GB VRAM at 64k: no headroom for 131k on the 96 GB card
default thinking mode leaks reasoning into content without a configured reasoning parser; tier config must disable thinking or configure the parser
single-stream decode (97 tok/s) still trails the gpt-oss-120b production baseline (183 tok/s prior measurement)

recommendations.highlights: full 131072 context verified (needle 11.9 s; 13.1 s full prefill — fastest measured 131k prefill), tools 20/20, session pass
caveats: 29.2 tok/s single-stream is modest; the fla/GDN hybrid path on sm_120 likely leaves performance on the table
intelligence 1/2 (matches fast baseline, below heavy baseline)
vendor benchmark claims were used only for candidate selection, never as measurements

recommendations.notes: Nemotron-3-Nano-30B (the only fast-track candidate that fully served) needs the PIECEWISE workaround. The clean re-measure (WSL 2.7.10, idle host) resolved the earlier TTFT erraticness (warm p50 1.68 s) and lifted intelligence to 1/2 — but 15 tok/s single-stream decode is far below the incumbent; keep experimental.

recommendations.status: keep experimental; watch for stable-release support
recommendations.notes: unsupported on vLLM 0.19 (NGC 26.04); loads and serves on nightly v0.23.1rc1 with native nemotron_v3 parser; 64k TTFT 3.1 s. Final tool-call cycle recorded in evidence.

recommendations.status: strongest measured challenger to the incumbent; NOT promoted
recommendations.highlights: intelligence 2/2 (incumbent: 1/2), tools 20/20, 64k window, ~147 tok/s decode at 178 ms warm TTFT
caveats: raw decode trails the incumbent's promotion-time 236 tok/s figure; llama.cpp MTP pass untested
graduation preflight (--needle-ctx within window) not yet run

environment_caveats: Measurements before 2026-07-11T06:15Z ran on WSL 2.6.2 (below the community-reported 2.7.0 cudagraph-reliability threshold for RTX 5090). The platform was upgraded to WSL 2.7.10 mid-run; Omni's final cycle and the Nemotron text re-measure ran post-upgrade and are labeled accordingly.

Some earlier measurements ran with concurrent model downloads/loads on the host; decode-bound throughput numbers are believed insensitive, but single-request TTFT numbers from that window carry a concurrency caveat (see failures.md and the finding narrative).
2026-07-11 extension measurements ran on WSL 2.7.10 throughout, one candidate resident per card, with an automated page-cache watchdog active.
